description_long: This is a roughly hewn stone block with an irregular, angular shape and a predominantly grayish-tan coloration. The surface texture varies, with one side displaying a series of small, star-shaped indentations, possibly from tooling or decorative work. The other faces are mostly flat but uneven, with visible chipping and natural stone grain. The block appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Subtle discoloration and weathering are present, especially along the edges and corners, suggesting outdoor exposure. Its size and form make it suitable as a barrier or obstacle in an urban or landscaped environment.
description: A dense, irregularly shaped gray-tan stone block with rough surfaces and one side featuring star-shaped indentations.
description_view_0: The front face is flat with subtle horizontal striations and a slightly sloped top edge.
description_view_1: This angled side reveals the block's thickness and rough, unworked stone texture.
description_view_2: A narrow, vertical face with some discoloration and minor pitting, showing the block's depth.
description_view_3: This view highlights the star-shaped indentations and a curved, beveled edge, suggesting decorative or functional tooling.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or landscaped park border
attribute_weathering_pattern: Edges and corners show minor chipping and surface discoloration, indicating moderate weathering from outdoor exposure.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: One side features star-shaped indentations, likely from tooling or decorative work.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: likely placed adjacent to or stacked with other blocks as a barrier or border
